title: Senegalia mirandae
taxonomy: Senegalia mirandae is classified under the family Fabaceae and is a species of legume. It is a member of the genus Senegalia, which is part of the Acacia genus in the subfamily Mimosoideae. The genus Senegalia was formerly known as Acacia, but it has been reclassified in recent years. This reclassification was due to the discovery of distinct differences in the morphology and anatomy of the Senegalia species compared to other Acacia species. The classification of Senegalia mirandae is still a subject of ongoing research and study in the field of botany.

intro: Senegalia mirandae, also known as the Mirandola tree, is a small to medium-sized tree native to Mexico, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Senegalia mirandae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Senegalia mirandae include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits in traditional medicine.
